results.title: Jem Cohen interviewed by Emma Crimmings
results.brief_description: Jem Cohen talks with Emma Crimmings about his relationship with cities and curating a secret museum of unseeen human activity. He describes the qualities and significance of Super 8 film and cameras. He talks about attempts to categorise his work and the limitations of terms such as ‘poetic’ or ‘experimental’. Works such as ‘Lost Book Found’ and ‘This is a History of New York’ are discussed as well as his methodology as an independent film-maker. The discussion also touches on his work with music video and shooting the conflict between the regional and global in Melbourne.

results.title: Pegged in
results.brief_description: The secret life of a peg! A comic animation of common wooden pegs who, once left alone in a laundry basket, come to life and sprout legs. This quirky short has the pegs getting caught up in a string, one burns itself of an iron, and they all conspire to release two of their fellow pegs from a shirt pocket, in the process cause chaos.

results.title: Federation Square time lapse project
results.brief_description: Time lapse footage of the construction of the Federation Square site. Includes hourly photos take from the architect’s office, commencing April 1998. Intended to be used for a short animation sequence.

results.title: (dis)LOCATIONs symposium
results.creator_credit: Anna Munster, Dennis Del Favero, Jeffrey Shaw and Peter Weibel
results.brief_description:
A conference about fragmentation of ‘community’, urbanisation and the collapse of locale and neighbourhood, the erosion of the private spaces of the sexual and familial. These have emerged as thematics attributable to the restructuring and divergent flows of new information economies and mediascapes.

Featuring key note speakers including Jeffrey Shaw, Dennis Del Favero, Agnes Hegedus, Susan Norrie, Peter Weibel, Ursula Frohne, Lev Manovich.

results.title: Payback
results.creator_credit: Warwick Thornton, Penny McDonald and Blackfella Films
results.brief_description: Paddy, an old aboriginal man, has been in jail for twenty years. It is the day of his release. Paddy knows of two laws - white law and black law. The twenty years he has spent doing time for the white man’s law has been in preparation for this one - his payback day.

results.title: Ballet robotique
results.brief_description: This dynamic and playful work examines the use of robots in American industry. Exploiting the visual joys of the kinetic robotic motion set to a soundtrack by the Royal Philharmonic Orchestra, this lightly humorous film show the industrial replacement of humans in tedious and hazardous tasks.
